target.name: Einride
target.founder: Robert Falck, Filip Lilja, Linnéa Kornehed

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Einride is a Series C autonomous trucking company in the Transportation sector, currently valued at $1.8B post-money with $752M+ total raised. The relevant comp universe consists of 7 private frontier-tech peers in Transportation, including Rivian, Harbinger, and ZeroAvia, though no public comparables exist in the narrow peer set.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation lever here. Applied to Einride's $1.8B last post-money valuation, a 1.7x step-up multiplier—standard for Series C to subsequent-stage progression—implies a forward valuation of $3.1B. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none yielded sufficient datapoints within same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters (minimum 3 required). Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of frontier-transportation subsectors.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.6B to $3.5B, centered on $3.1B. This range reflects reasonable band-widths around a single, well-grounded methodology. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking timelines, regulatory pathway clarity for autonomous operations, and capital efficiency relative to peer burn profiles—data absent from this valuation set. The median estimate should be treated as indicative pending deeper operational and market-traction diligence.
